title: Aggregation, Grouping, HAVING, and Honest Metrics · Result-Grain Case: Metric Validation
summary: A service lead needs ticket counts and response effort by team without double counting or hiding teams with no work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql metric validation increment in an audited service-metrics query pack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reconcile totals, denominators, missing groups, and changed cases before publishing an aggregate claim.
Produce multiple defensible measures with CASE or FILTER while preserving one clear denominator.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 3 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure. A defensible solution applies Metric Validation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an audited service-metrics query pack.

content.code:
WITH effort AS (
  SELECT ticket_id, SUM(minutes_spent) AS minutes
  FROM ticket_events
  GROUP BY ticket_id
)
SELECT teams.name, COUNT(tickets.ticket_id) AS tickets,
       COALESCE(SUM(effort.minutes), 0) AS minutes
FROM teams
LEFT JOIN tickets ON tickets.team_id = teams.team_id
LEFT JOIN effort ON effort.ticket_id = tickets.ticket_id
GROUP BY teams.team_id, teams.name
ORDER BY teams.name;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-metric-validation-rzfgth
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running

FROM tickets
GROUP BY team_id
ORDER BY team_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
